front: Building: skyscraper in Malmo, Sweden — nine twisted pentagonal segments with the top floor rotated 90 degrees from the base
Building: Swedish skyscraper based on the architect's own marble sculpture of a twisting human form; developer Johnny Örbäck commissioned it after seeing that sculpture

back: Quadracci Pavilion, Milwaukee Art Museum (Santiago Calatrava)

front: Building: Milwaukee Art Museum pavilion — Burke Brise Soleil of 72 movable white steel fin-like wings that open and close like a bird
back: Quadracci Pavilion, Milwaukee Art Museum (Santiago Calatrava)

front: Building: ship-like museum addition; Windhover Hall whose chancel is shaped like the prow of a ship; sits alongside Eero Saarinen's War Memorial Center
back: Quadracci Pavilion, Milwaukee Art Museum (Santiago Calatrava)
World Trade Center Transportation Hub / Oculus (Santiago Calatrava)
work: World Trade Center Transportation Hub (Oculus), New York City

front: Building: NYC transportation hub whose white steel ribs form wings meant to evoke a bird released from a child's hands; criticized for poor functionality
back: World Trade Center Transportation Hub / Oculus (Santiago Calatrava)
work: World Trade Center Transportation Hub (Oculus), New York City

back: City of Arts and Sciences, Valencia (Santiago Calatrava)

front: Building: cultural complex in Valencia co-designed with Felix Candela; built in the former bed of the Turia River; IMAX theater with concrete 'eyelid' that opens to reveal a pool
back: City of Arts and Sciences, Valencia (Santiago Calatrava)

front: Building: Valencia opera house named for Queen Sofia; architect was sued after tile mosaics were blown off; nicknamed 'the white elephant'
back: City of Arts and Sciences, Valencia (Santiago Calatrava)

front: Building: Puente del Alamillo (Seville) — harp-like, asymmetric cantilever-spar cable-stayed bridge built for the 1992 World's Fair on Isla de la Cartuja
Building: Zubizuri in Bilbao — white tied-arch footbridge; Chords Bridge / Bridge of Strings in Jerusalem; Sundial Bridge at Turtle Bay, Redding, California
Building: Samuel Beckett Bridge (Dublin) — resembles a harp laid on its side; 31 cables representing the strings; rotates 90 degrees to allow boats through the Liffey

Building: Dublin bridge formerly called the Macken Street Bridge; lies two miles downstream from the James Joyce Bridge; Tom Lane recorded cable sounds with contact microphones for a 2014 'river cantata'
Building: Constitution Bridge crossing Venice's Grand Canal — criticized as out of place; retrofitted spherical wheelchair lift overheated and was scrapped
Architect: Spanish architect and structural engineer born 1951 in Valencia; known for curvilinear organic structures and cantilever-spar cable-stayed bridges
Architect: Gare de Lyon Saint-Exupery (Lyon) in form of a bird in flight; Gare do Oriente (Lisbon) has a 'forest' of steel trees above; Stadelhofen Station (Zurich) has underground arcade
Architect: communications tower on Montjuic for the 1992 Barcelona Olympics doubles as a sundial; designed the Athens 2004 Olympic Velodrome and a stadium with a retractable glass roof
Architect: designed Bilbao Airport's main terminal; proposed (unrealized) redesigns for Genoa's Morandi Bridge after its partial collapse
